title: littorella australis
intro:
Littorella australis, also known as New Zealand duckweed, is a small, floating aquatic plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its tiny leaves and ability to thrive in shallow water.

The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

Research on Littorella australis has revealed its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce oxidative stress, alleviate inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various health issues, although its efficacy and safety for human consumption have not been extensively studied.
